text.title: Type Checker Configuration
text.summary: mypy strict config, pyright config, Django-stubs wiring, and ruff ANN/TCH/UP rules for annotation coverage.

section.title: mypy Strict Configuration

rule.statement: Use `strict = true` in `[tool.mypy]`. For legacy codebases, add per-module overrides to opt modules in one by one — never reduce strictness repo-wide to fix a count of errors.
rule.rationale: `strict = true` enables: `disallow_untyped_defs`, `disallow_any_generics`, `warn_return_any`, `no_implicit_optional`, `check_untyped_defs`, and others. Reducing it repo-wide re-hides exactly the bugs mypy was meant to catch.

rule.statement: Add `[[tool.mypy.overrides]]` entries to exclude `migrations.*` (generated code) and relax `tests.*` (dynamic fixtures). Exclude packages without stubs via `ignore_missing_imports = true` scoped to those packages.
rule.rationale: Django migrations trigger false positives under strict mode — they use dynamic field references mypy cannot resolve. Tests use pytest fixtures that produce `untyped-def` noise. Scoped ignores keep signal high for production code.

section.reference: Full mypy strict config with Django-stubs and override sections

example.note: mypy strict config with scoped overrides
example.code:
[tool.mypy] python_version = "3.12" strict = true warn_unused_ignores = true
plugins = ["mypy_django_plugin.main"]
[tool.mypy.plugins.django-stubs] django_settings_module = "myproject.settings"
[[tool.mypy.overrides]] module = "tests.*" disallow_untyped_defs = false
[[tool.mypy.overrides]] module = "migrations.*" ignore_errors = true
[[tool.mypy.overrides]] module = ["boto3.*", "botocore.*"] ignore_missing_imports = true

rule.statement: Use pyright in VS Code via the Pylance extension (IDE-time feedback). Use mypy in CI for strict gate. Running both on the same codebase catches the union of both checkers' error sets.
rule.rationale: pyright is 3-5x faster than mypy and runs incrementally in the editor. mypy is stricter on some PEPs and has the django-stubs plugin ecosystem. Both together find more errors than either alone.

example.code: [tool.pyright] pythonVersion = "3.12" typeCheckingMode = "strict" reportMissingImports = true reportMissingTypeStubs = false reportUnusedImport = true reportUnusedVariable = true

rule.statement: Enable ruff rule groups `ANN` (annotation coverage), `TCH` (type-checking imports), and `UP` (pyupgrade) in CI. Use `UP` with `--fix` in pre-commit to auto-upgrade deprecated typing syntax.
rule.rationale: `ANN` flags functions missing return or argument annotations. `TCH` moves runtime-unnecessary imports into `if TYPE_CHECKING:` blocks (reduces startup time). `UP` auto-converts `Optional[X]` → `X | None`, `List[X]` → `list[X]`, etc.

rule.statement: Install `django-stubs` and `djangorestframework-stubs` and wire the mypy plugin. Without the plugin, mypy cannot understand `Model.objects`, `QuerySet` generics, or DRF serializer field resolution.
rule.rationale: The django-stubs mypy plugin transforms Django's dynamic ORM API into statically-typed equivalents. Without it, every `Model.objects.filter()` call has type `Any`, eliminating mypy's coverage of the entire data layer.

example.note: Typed QuerySet return from service function
